title: Record properties should not have public setters
summary: Records exist to model immutable values: equality, with expressions, and safe sharing all assume the value cannot change after construction. A property with a public set accessor breaks that assumption, so two records that were equal can silently diverge and hashed lookups can lose track of them.
Declare the property with init or make it get-only and set it through the constructor.
messageFormat: Replace this 'set' accessor with 'init' so the record stays immutable.
